message:
style(web): improve desktop sidebar contrast and settings select specificity

## components.css
- Increase CSS specificity for .settings-select by adding .input prefix
  (.input.settings-select) to ensure it overrides base input styles
- Add flex: 0 0 auto to prevent the select from growing/shrinking in
  flex containers, maintaining consistent width regardless of siblings
- This fixes layout issues where settings dropdowns would expand to fill
  available space unexpectedly

## desktop-ui.css
Comprehensive contrast and color improvements for the desktop sidebar:

- .sidebar-panel: Change background from --color-background-elevated to
  --color-background-alt for better visual separation from main content;
  use --color-border-light instead of --color-border-subtle for clearer
  panel boundaries

- .tool-button, .category-button: Change from --color-text-muted to
  --color-text-dim for slightly increased contrast while maintaining
  the inactive/secondary visual hierarchy

- .tool-item: Change from --color-text-dim to --color-text for active
  items, making selected tools more prominent and easier to identify

- .category-chevron: Use --color-text-dim for collapse/expand indicators,
  aligning with other secondary UI elements

- .shortcut-hint: Use --color-border-light and --color-text-dim for
  keyboard shortcut badges, improving readability without being distracting

These changes improve visual hierarchy and accessibility by ensuring
sufficient contrast ratios throughout the sidebar navigation, particularly
benefiting users in low-light environments or with reduced contrast
sensitivity.
